steps.title: Keep subject and verb together
steps.content: The blank finishes the prepositional phrase of Connecticut and then needs the verb for the subject Olympia Brown. Standard punctuation should not separate a subject from its verb.

steps.title: Remove boundary breaks
steps.content: A makes Played an important role a fragment, C puts a semicolon between subject and verb, and D puts a colon where no independent clause precedes it. The sentence needs no punctuation between Connecticut and played. The answer is B.
